effective-stress.title: Effective Stress
effective-stress.theory: [JA] 有効応力 is the portion of 全応力 carried by the soil skeleton — not the pore water. It controls ALL mechanical behavior: せん断強さ, 圧密, and 液状化 resistance.
effective-stress.explanation: [JA] When soil is loaded, pore water initially carries the load (excess pore pressure). As water drains, 応力 transfers to the soil skeleton (有効応力 increases). This is 圧密.
effective-stress.example: [JA] A 粘土 layer at 5m depth with water table at surface: σ = 18×5 = 90 kPa, u = 10×5 = 50 kPa, σ' = 40 kPa. After drainage: σ' increases → strength increases.

shear-strength.title: Shear Strength
shear-strength.theory: [JA] The maximum shear 応力 a soil can withstand before failure. Governed by モール・クーロン criterion. Depends on 有効応力 (drained) or 全応力 (undrained).
shear-strength.explanation: [JA] c' = effective 粘着力 (kPa) — true bonding between particles. φ' = 摩擦角 (°) — depends on particle shape, mineralogy. 砂: c'≈0, φ'=28-42°. 粘土: c'>0, φ'=20-30°.
shear-strength.example: [JA] Slope failure in 粘土: τ along failure surface reaches τf. Stability number Ns = cu/γH. For rotational slip: 安全率 = ΣResisting moments / ΣDriving moments.

consolidation.title: Consolidation
consolidation.theory: [JA] Time-dependent compression of saturated fine-grained soils as excess 間隙水圧 dissipates. テルツァーギ 1D theory applies to normally consolidated 粘土s.
consolidation.explanation: [JA] Tv = time factor (dimensionless). cv = coefficient of 圧密 (m²/year). H = drainage path (half layer for double drainage). U = degree of 圧密.
consolidation.example: [JA] 5m 粘土 layer, cv = 2 m²/year, double drainage. Time for 90% 圧密: T90 = 0.848, t = 0.848×(2.5)²/2 = 2.65 years.

liquefaction.title: Liquefaction
liquefaction.theory: [JA] Saturated loose 砂y soils lose 有効応力 (and thus strength) during cyclic 地震 loading. Excess pore pressure builds up until u = σ, causing σ' = 0 — soil behaves like a liquid.
liquefaction.explanation: [JA] FL < 1.0: Liquefiable. FL = 1.0–1.5: Marginal. FL > 1.5: Safe. Critical for Japan — the 1964 Niigata 地震, 1995 Kobe, and 2011 Tohoku all caused massive 液状化.
liquefaction.example: [JA] Niigata (1964): Showa Bridge collapsed due to 液状化 of loose river 砂 deposits. amax=0.16g, N<10 砂s with water table at 1m depth → σ'v very small → FL<1 in most areas.

p-wave.title: P-Wave (Primary / Compressional)
p-wave.theory: [JA] P波s are compressional body waves. Particle motion is parallel to propagation direction (push-pull). Fastest 地震波. Can travel through solids AND liquids/gases.
p-wave.explanation: [JA] Typical Vp values: Air 340 m/s. Water 1500 m/s. Loose 砂 200-800 m/s. Dense 砂 800-2000 m/s. 岩 3000-6000 m/s. Saturated soil: Vp dominated by water (≈1500 m/s).
p-wave.example: [JA] 屈折法 survey: Layer 1 Vp1=400 m/s (loose fill), Layer 2 Vp2=1600 m/s (saturated 砂), Layer 3 Vp3=4000 m/s (bed岩). Crossover distances determine layer depths.

s-wave.title: S-Wave (Shear / Secondary)
s-wave.theory: [JA] S波s are shear body waves. Particle motion is perpendicular to propagation direction. Cannot travel through liquids. Fundamental for 地盤応答 analysis and Vs30 calculation.
s-wave.explanation: [JA] Vs is the KEY parameter for geotechnical site characterization. Measured by PS検層, 表面波探査, seismic コーン貫入試験, crosshole. Vs30 = average Vs in top 30m, used for Japanese seismic design code site classification.
s-wave.example: [JA] Soft 粘土: Vs = 80-150 m/s. Dense 砂: Vs = 200-400 m/s. Soft 岩: Vs = 400-700 m/s. Hard 岩: Vs > 700 m/s. Site Class D (告示): Vs30 < 150 m/s → high amplification.

spt.title: Standard Penetration Test (SPT)
spt.theory: [JA] The 標準貫入試験 uses a 63.5 kg hammer falling 760 mm to drive a split-spoon sampler 150+300 mm. The blow count for the last 300 mm = N値. Most common soil investigation in Japan.
spt.explanation: [JA] N値 interpretation: N<4: very loose/soft. N=4-10: loose. N=10-30: medium dense. N=30-50: dense. N>50: very dense/岩. Japanese practice uses rod energy ratio ER ≈ 72% (automatic hammer).

cpt.title: CPTu (Cone Penetration Test with Pore Pressure)
cpt.theory: [JA] コーン貫入試験u continuously measures tip resistance (qc), sleeve friction (fs), and pore pressure (u2) as a cone penetrates at 20 mm/s. Provides continuous soil profile without disturbance.
cpt.explanation: [JA] Ic < 1.31: 礫ly 砂. 1.31-2.05: Clean 砂. 2.05-2.60: シルトy 砂. 2.60-2.95: 砂y シルト. 2.95-3.60: シルトy 粘土. >3.60: 粘土. Pore pressure Bq = (u2-u0)/(qt-σv).
cpt.example: [JA] At 10m: qc=5 MPa, fs=80 kPa, u2=120 kPa. σv=180 kPa, σ'v=100 kPa, u0=80 kPa. qt=5.03 MPa. Qt=48.5. Fr=1.68%. Ic=2.11 → 砂y シルト/シルトy 砂 boundary.

ps-logging.title: PS Logging (Downhole Seismic)
ps-logging.theory: [JA] PS検層 measures P and S wave travel times in a ボーリング孔 using a downhole receiver and surface source. Provides accurate velocity profile for Vs30 and dynamic property calculation.
ps-logging.explanation: [JA] Downhole: single receiver moved down ボーリング孔, source at surface offset 1-2m. Interval method: use first arrival times at each depth. Crosshole: source and receiver in separate ボーリング孔s — better for soft layers.
ps-logging.example: [JA] 10m depth: Δt_P = 14 ms, Δt_S = 32 ms for 1m interval. Vp = 1/0.014 = 71 m/s? No — typical readings: arrival at 10m vs 11m: ΔtP=2ms → Vp=500 m/s. ΔtS=5ms → Vs=200 m/s. G0 = 1800×200² = 72 MPa.

seismic-refraction.title: Seismic Refraction Survey
seismic-refraction.theory: [JA] Surface seismic method that uses first-arrival travel times to map subsurface velocity layers. Waves refract (bend) at interfaces where velocity increases with depth — Snell's Law.
seismic-refraction.explanation: [JA] Geophone array (24-48 channels). Source: sledgehammer or seismic gun. V1 < V2 required (velocity increase with depth). Cannot detect velocity inversions (hidden layers). Used to map bed岩 depth, water table, fill thickness.
seismic-refraction.example: [JA] Survey result: V1=400 m/s (fill), V2=1600 m/s (saturated 砂), V3=4000 m/s (bed岩). Crossover distances: xc12 = 2h1√((V2-V1)/(V2+V1)). Bed岩 depth = 12m. Used for tunnel route assessment.

resistivity.title: Electrical Resistivity Survey
resistivity.theory: [JA] Measures bulk electrical 比抵抗 of the ground to map geological structures. Four-electrode arrays: Wenner, Schlumberger, dipole-dipole. Injects current (AB), measures voltage (MN).
resistivity.explanation: [JA] Typical resistivities: Sea water 0.2 Ωm. Saturated 粘土 1-20 Ωm. Saturated 砂 20-200 Ωm. Dry 砂 500-10000 Ωm. Granite 1000-10000 Ωm. Used for: saltwater intrusion, buried waste, fault detection, 地下水.
resistivity.example: [JA] Coastal site: 2D 比抵抗 section shows zone ρ<5 Ωm at 10-20m depth → saltwater intrusion in 砂 aquifer. Used to define freshwater/saltwater interface for well placement.

masw.title: MASW Surface Wave Survey
masw.jp: [JA] 表面波探査・微動探査
masw.theory: [JA] 表面波探査 (Multichannel Analysis of Surface Waves) measures Rayleigh wave dispersion to invert for Vs profile. Microtremor H/V ratio gives site fundamental frequency. Both methods are non-invasive — no ボーリング孔 required.
masw.explanation: [JA] 表面波探査: 24-channel array, 4.5 Hz geophones, 2-5 m spacing. Sledgehammer source at one end. Dispersion curve extracted from f-k analysis → inversion → Vs(z) profile. Microtremor uses ambient noise recorded with 3-component seismometer — no active source needed.
masw.example: [JA] H/V survey at Tokyo soft ground: f0 = 1.0 Hz → site period T = 1.0 s → thick soft deposits (~40m). H/V amplitude > 4 → strong seismic amplification expected. 表面波探査 confirms Vs30 = 140 m/s → 第3種地盤 classification.

borehole-camera.title: Borehole Camera Investigation
borehole-camera.theory: [JA] A ボアホールカメラ (孔内カメラ) is a waterproof optical probe lowered into a ボーリング孔 to directly observe the ボーリング孔 wall. It provides visual images or video of 岩 and soil features that cannot be captured by core recovery alone. Used in both soil and 岩 investigations.
borehole-camera.explanation: [JA] Modern ボアホールカメラs provide 360° panoramic images at any depth. They reveal fracture orientation, aperture (crack width), weathering condition, joint infill material, and 地下水 inflow locations. Camera data is essential for confirming what the core log shows — and detecting features lost during drilling.
borehole-camera.example: [JA] Tunnel project in Hokkaido granite. ボアホールカメラ at 30–50m depth shows steeply dipping fractures (dip 70°) oriented toward planned tunnel face. Camera reveals open joints with water inflow at 42m. Design changed: increased shotcrete thickness + additional 岩 bolts. Without camera, this risk would have been missed.

pressuremeter.title: Pressuremeter Test (PMT)
pressuremeter.theory: [JA] The プレッシャーメーター test inflates a cylindrical probe inside a ボーリング孔 to measure the in-situ lateral 応力-ひずみ relationship of the soil or 岩. It directly measures horizontal deformation behavior — something no other standard test provides. Based on the Ménard プレッシャーメーター (1956).
pressuremeter.explanation: [JA] The probe is inserted into a pre-drilled ボーリング孔 at the test depth. Inflation pressure is increased in steps while measuring volume change. Three phases: (1) ボーリング孔 wall reopening, (2) pseudo-elastic expansion, (3) plastic zone growth. Em is taken from the linear elastic phase. PL is the pressure at which soil fails (limit pressure).
pressuremeter.example: [JA] Soft 岩 tunnel portal in Kyushu. プレッシャーメーター tests at 3m, 5m, 8m depths. Results: Em = 15, 22, 35 MPa. PL = 0.8, 1.2, 2.1 MPa. Foundation design uses Em/3 ≈ 5-12 MPa as working 弾性係数. プレッシャーメーター data more reliable than lab testing for weathered 岩 because sample disturbance is minimized.

rqd.title: Rock Quality Designation (RQD)
rqd.theory: [JA] 岩盤品質指標 (岩 Quality Designation) quantifies the degree of jointing or fracturing in a 岩 mass. It is measured from drill core as the percentage of intact core pieces longer than 100mm in a given drill run. Developed by Deere (1963) and now the standard for 岩 mass classification worldwide.
rqd.explanation: [JA] Only natural fractures are counted — mechanical breaks from drilling are excluded. 岩盤品質指標 rating: 90-100% Excellent (優良), 75-90% Good (良), 50-75% Fair (普通), 25-50% Poor (不良), <25% Very Poor (極めて不良). 岩盤品質指標 feeds into RMR (Bieniawski 1989) and the NGI Q-system for tunnel support design.
rqd.example: [JA] Core run in granite tunnel site (Osaka): 2.0m run. Intact pieces (natural breaks only): 8cm, 25cm, 35cm, 42cm, 32cm, 28cm, 15cm = sum 7 pieces. Pieces ≥ 100mm: 25, 35, 42, 32, 28 cm = 162cm. 岩盤品質指標 = 162/200 × 100% = 81% → GOOD. Tunnel support: spot bolts + mesh. No heavy steel sets needed.

plate-load-test.title: Plate Load Test
plate-load-test.theory: [JA] The 平板載荷試験 directly measures the 支持力 and deformation 弾性係数 of soil or 岩 at the foundation level. A rigid steel plate (typically 300mm or 750mm diameter) is loaded in increments while measuring 沈下. It is the most direct measurement of ground response under real foundation conditions.
plate-load-test.explanation: [JA] The test is performed at the design foundation level in a pit or directly on the ground surface. A hydraulic jack applies load through the steel plate onto the soil. 沈下 is measured with dial gauges or LVDTs. Load-沈下 curve defines: elastic zone, yield point, ultimate 支持力. Japanese road design uses K30 as the primary subgrade parameter.
plate-load-test.example: [JA] Road subgrade test in Saitama. 300mm plate on compacted fill. Load applied in 5 steps: 50, 100, 150, 200, 250 kPa. 沈下 at 250 kPa: 12mm. K30 = (250 kPa at s=1.25mm): K30 = p(at 1.25mm)/1.25mm × 1000 = 120 MN/m³. Japanese specification: K30 ≥ 110 MN/m³ for road base → PASS. E0 = (1-0.3²)×0.785×p×0.3/s = ~65 MPa.

gpr.title: Ground Penetrating Radar (GPR)
gpr.theory: [JA] Ground Penetrating Radar emits short pulses of electromagnetic energy into the ground and records the reflections from subsurface interfaces. The reflection time (two-way travel time) and wave velocity allow calculation of depth to reflectors. 地中レーダー is the primary non-invasive method for detecting shallow features: buried utilities, voids, rebar, and geological boundaries.
gpr.explanation: [JA] 地中レーダー antennas range from 25 MHz (deep penetration, low resolution) to 2 GHz (high resolution, shallow). In Japan, 200-500 MHz antennas are most common for geotechnical work. 地中レーダー works best in dry, resistive soils. Wet 粘土 and seawater-saturated ground severely attenuate the signal. Output is a radargram showing reflection hyperbolas from point targets (pipes, rebars) and continuous reflectors (layer boundaries).
gpr.example: [JA] Pre-construction utility survey in Tokyo. 500 MHz antenna survey reveals: water pipe at 0.8m depth (hyperbolic reflection), old gas line at 1.2m (metallic reflection, strong), unknown void at 2.1m depth (bright reflection from top, no signal below). 地中レーダー data allows safe excavation planning. Also used for bridge deck rebar detection and tunnel lining inspection in Japan.
